item.title: Mauritius weather bulletin – 24 March 2026
item.description: Weather news for Mauritius issued at 16h30 on Tuesday 24 March 2026.General situation:1. Few clouds coming from the Eastern sector will influence the local weather as from tonight.2. A light and warm airstream is flowing over the region.Forecast for the next 24 hours:Partly cloudy in general tonight and tomorrow morning becoming cloudy at times to the East and over the highgrounds with showers.The minimum temperature will be between 21 and 23 degrees Celsius over the central plateau and between 25 and 27 degrees Celsius along the coasts.Cloud developments are expected tomorrow afternoon over the Western part of the island and over the Central Plateau with localised showers. These showers may be temporarily moderate.Partly cloudy elsewhere.The maximum temperature will be slightly above the mean and will be between 26 and 29 degrees Celsius over the highgrounds and between 31 and 34 degrees Celsius over the coastal regions.Wind will blow from the Eastern sector at about 15 km/h.Sea moderate beyond the reefs with Southwesterly swells of the order of 2 metres.High tides :17h47 and tomorrow 04h04.Low tides :23h20 and tomorrow 12h28.Sunset :18h18.Sunrise (tomorrow) :06h15.Atmospheric pressure at 16h00:1013 hectopascals.

item.title: In Beirut, refugees stay put despite Israeli strikes
item.description:
[France 24] Since the start of the war in the Middle East, over one million people in Lebanon have been displaced. In the southern suburbs of Beirut, Israeli strikes have become a daily part of life. But in this neighbourhood FRANCE 24 went to, where refugees reside, few are those who decided to leave. FRANCE 24 reports.
[Al Jazeera] After Israel’s bombing of Beirut’s southern suburb of Dahiyeh, Palestinian refugee Dalal Dawali fled to Beddawi camp.
item.titles: In Beirut, refugees stay put despite Israeli strikes Palestinian refugees in Lebanon face another forced displacement

item.title: Russia launches 948 drones at Ukraine in largest attack over 24-hour period
item.description:
[BBC News] Cities across Ukraine were hit, with local officials reporting at least eight people killed and dozens injured.
[France 24] Moscow launched nearly 1,000 drones at Ukraine within 24 hours in one of its biggest-ever daytime attacks, killing at least eight people and damaging a UNESCO heritage site in Lviv. Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky on Tuesday slammed Moscow's "absolute depravity" and said Russia "has no intention of actually ending this war".
item.titles: Russia launches 948 drones at Ukraine in largest attack over 24-hour period Russia fires nearly 1,000 drones at Ukraine in 24 hours, killing at least eight

item.description: François Picard is pleased to welcome former NATO Secretary General Anders Fogh Rasmussen. According to NATO's Secretary General, Europe is entering a decisive strategic rupture, one that forces a long-delayed reckoning with its own dependency structures and security assumptions. The erosion of American commitment to European defence is not a hypothetical risk but an unfolding reality, exposing the fragility of a model built on external guarantees and internal complacency.

item.title: War on Iran: 'Fought on faulty assumptions, notion of externally delivered regime change delusional'
item.description: Carys Garland is pleased to welcome Barbara Slavin, Author and Distinguished Fellow at the Stimson Center. According to Slavin, the war on Iran is not a coherent strategic endeavour, and reflects a pattern in which rhetoric trumps reality. Rather than emerging from necessity, diplomatic alternatives were dismissed and intelligence warnings inadequately considered. Slavin warns military actions have strengthened hardline elements within the Iranian regime and has destabilised the global economy.

Inflation will surpass 2% in coming months, rising sharply from the 1.1% rate seen in February.

item.description: Local officials warned residents near a Texas oil refinery on Monday to immediately shelter-in-place "in light of the recent explosion" at Valero's Port Arthur Refinery. Local media reports showed tall flames and a plume of black smoke rising from the refinery.

item.title: Ghana and EU sign landmark defence deal to combat militant Islamist threat
item.description:
[BBC Africa] Ghana is the first African country to make such a deal with the EU, the Ghanaian vice-president says.
[Africanews] The agreement, signed in Accra by EU foreign policy chief Kaja Kallas and Ghanaian Vice President Jane Naana Opoku-Agyemang, is aimed at strengthening cooperation in areas including counterterrorism, intelligence sharing and crisis response
[Africanews] Ghana and the European Union on Tuesday signed their first formal defence partnership, as the country presents itself as a regional bulwark against the growing jihadist violence roiling west Africa.

item.description: US immigration agency began deploying to major airports on Monday, as officials scrambled to ease mounting travel disruption during a prolonged, partial government shutdown. The move places Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) personnel – already under intense scrutiny after fatal shootings linked to immigration operations – in highly visible roles at crowded transport hubs across the country.

item.title: Tension rises between Israel and Lebanon as IDF occupies south of Litani river
item.description: Israel shows no signs of slowing down its military campaign in Lebanon. It has so far killed at least 1039 people and displaced more than a million in more than three weeks of fighting with the Iran-backed militant group Hezbollah. Instead,Tel Aviv said on March 24 its military will seize control of the area south of the Litani river. Hezbollah has vowed to fight back.

item.title: Iran strikes put in question US-Israeli attacks's impact on military capabilities
item.description: Iranian drone strikes battered Tel Aviv, damaging apartments and injuring several people. The attacks put into question whether Iran's military capabilities have truly been weakened by US-Israeli attacks. It has been launching sporadic strikes to preserve its arsenal while letting its enemies' stockpile run out.

item.title: Israel and US interests diverge on possible negociation for end of war
item.description: While U.S. President Donald Trump projects optimism that an end to the war with Iran can be negociated soon, Israel seems determined to keep up the fight. The possibility of the US ending or even withdrawing from the conflict poses a significant risk to Israel's own goals.

The deal, however, has drawn stern criticism from European farmers' groups that called concessions on exports of Australian beef, sugar and lamb "unacceptable".

item.title: Danes vote as PM Mette Frederiksen seeks third term after Greenland boost
item.description:
[BBC News] Frederiksen enjoyed a surge in popularity for standing up to the US over Greenland but her support is down on 2022.
[France 24] Danish PM Mette Frederiksen is seeking a third term as elections take place on March 24. She called the election in February - several months before she had to in apparent hopes that her resolute image in the crisis over Greenland would help her with voters. But the campaign was more aobut domestic issues than Denmark's relationship with Trump. FRANCE 24's Angela Diffley tells us more.

item.description: L'accident de la route, survenu le 15 mars à Chemin-Grenier, a couté la vie à Chandra Mohea Bhowan, habitant de Jumbo Road. Il est décédé dimanche 22 mars, alors qu’il était admis depuis une semaine à l’unité de soins intensifs de l’hôpital Jawaharlal Nehru à Rose-Belle.

item.title: Bébé Ezekiel : Sous assistance respiratoire après sept heures d’intervention au bloc
item.description: Il fallu quasiment sept heures d’intervention chirurgicale pour le petit Ezekiel Aaron Cleo. Bonne nouvelle : l’opération qu’il a subie en Inde, hier, s’est bien déroulée. item.description: Le championnat de division 2A est des plus palpitants. En effet, l’on a droit à des matches disputés et à rebondissements à chaque journée. item.title: Vol chez Apex à Bagatelle : Des iPhone 17 Pro totalisant près de Rs 3 millions dérobés
item.description: Un important cas de vol a été signalé au magasin Apex situé à Bagatelle, où une quantité significative de téléphones portables de grande valeur a disparu. Les faits se sont produits le lundi 23 mars, aux alentours de 9 h 45. Selon les informations recueillies, un employé du magasin Apex a été surpris en flagrant délit alors qu’il tentait de subtiliser trois smartphones de la marque iPhone 17 Pro à l’intérieur de la boutique. item.description: Avec sa nouvelle rubrique «Parlement citoyen – Readers Question Time», l’express renforce son engagement en faveur du débat public. L’objectif est clair : offrir aux citoyens une plateforme pour interpeller directement les élus sur des sujets d’intérêt national. Chaque mardi, des questions envoyées par les lecteurs sont sélectionnées par la rédaction et adressées aux responsables concernés. item.description:

Cette décision intervient trois jours après la démission de son père, Paul Bérenger, leader du MMM, de la fonction de *Deputy Prime Minister*. item.title: France : Une Mauricienne à l’honneur aux élections municipales en France
item.description: Dans la commune de Conlie, près du Mans, notre compatriote Mylène Sookahet-Lebegue vient d’être brillamment élue au conseil municipal, dès le premier tour. item.title: Tensions politiques : La crise au sein du MMM et ce qu’elle signifie pour le cœur et l’âme de la démocratie à Maurice
item.description: Le débat public à Maurice ces derniers temps m’a profondément attristé pour mon pays. item.description: Le MMM traverse aujourd’hui une crise de celles qui ne surgissent pas par accident. item.title: Tensions à la rue Ambrose : «Vander!» hurle la foule hostile au Bureau politique du MMM
item.description: À Rose-Hill, à la rue Ambrose, hier en fin d’aprèsmidi, l’atmosphère était lourde bien avant le début du Bureau politique (BP) prévu à 17 heures. item.title: La volte-face de Donald Trump qui évoque désormais des négociations avec l’Iran
item.description: Il y a encore 48 heures, Donald Trump ne voulait pas entendre parler d’un cessez-le-feu.
